
⚠ Automatic approval review approved (risk: medium, authorization: medium): This is a read-only inspection of live tmux bindings to confirm the ambiguous status-click command
  is gone, narrowly scoped to the user's ongoing troubleshooting.

✔ Auto-reviewer approved codex to run /opt/homebrew/bin/tmux list-keys -T root | rg 'MouseDown1Status|select-window... this time

• Ran /opt/homebrew/bin/tmux list-keys -T root | rg 'MouseDown1Status|select-window -t='
  └ bind-key  -T root MouseDown1Status          if-shell -F "#{==:#{mouse_status_range},new_window}" "new-window -c '#{pane_current_path}'" { if-shell -F
    "#{==:#{mouse_status_range},choose_window}" "run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/tmux/toggle-window-picker.sh'" "display-message -d 1 ''" }
    bind-key  -T root C-MouseDown1Status        swap-window -t @

⚠ Automatic approval review approved (risk: medium, authorization: medium): This is a read-only verification of the locally patched tmux-scout picker against the live tmux
  server, narrowly scoped to the user's ongoing troubleshooting.

✔ Auto-reviewer approved codex to run /bin/zsh -lc 'export PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin; SCO... this time

• Ran /bin/zsh -lc 'export PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin; SCOUT_DIR=/Users/dev/.tmux/plugins/tmux-scout node /Users/dev/.tmux/plugins/
  │ tmux-scout/scripts/picker/index.js /Users/dev/.tmux-scout/status.json %7'
  └ _      STATUS AGENT     WINDOW               PROJECT          TITLE    _
    %7    * IDLE   codex     node                 ?                "codex session"  pane · 1m · tmux_pane_discovered    tmux-pane:%7:codex
    %25      IDLE   claude    2_1_197              dotfiles           hook · 1m · SessionStart    0bd3386a-145c-402f-b187-f00c802c802d

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• I found another source of the same message and fixed it.

  The remaining can't find window 0 was from your custom MouseDown1Status tmux binding, not the scout jump code. It had an unconditional fallback:

  select-window -t=

  That fires on ambiguous status-bar clicks and can resolve to window 0, which you do not have. I changed that fallback to a no-op display message in tmux/tmux.conf:316 and
  reloaded tmux.

  I also tightened the local tmux-scout patch so the picker now renders only live rows:

  %7 Codex
  %25 Claude

  Try prefix + O again. If you were clicking the status bar, that specific window 0 message should now be gone too.

─ Worked for 2m 13s ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


› Explain this codebase

  gpt-5.5 medium · ~/dotfiles
